response_json.rationale: High NAND wear (95% wear leveling), elevated media/read/write errors indicate imminent failure risk despite missing TTF/latency data.

atomic_claims.claim: Media errors detected (median 60)
Extreme NAND wear (wear_leveling_count=95)
High read error rate (median 80)
Elevated write errors (median 40)
Combined SMART signals indicate deteriorating flash reliability

response_json.summary: SSD shows critical degradation indicators including 95% wear leveling count (near end-of-life threshold), elevated media/read/write errors, and 10k operational hours. Combined signals suggest advanced NAND wear and unstable storage behavior.
key_risks: Imminent failure risk due to 95% wear_leveling_count (near maximum endurance cycles)
Persistent media errors (60 counts) indicating physical cell degradation
Unstable I/O performance with 80 read errors and 40 write errors
Extended 10,000 power-on hours potentially accelerating component aging

atomic_claims.claim: Wear leveling count at 95% indicates SSD is near manufacturer-defined endurance limits
Media error count (60) exceeds normal background error correction capabilities
Read error rate (80) suggests developing issues with data retrieval reliability
Write error rate (40) indicates instability in data programming operations
10,000 power-on hours demonstrate extended drive usage contributing to component stress

recommendations.action: Migrate data and replace SSD immediately
recommendations.justification: Wear_leveling_count at 95% indicates critical NAND wear (IR:AF_wear_leveling_count). Combined with multiple error types (media/read/write errors > 0), this matches failure precursor patterns from SSD reliability studies
Persistent media errors (IR:AF_media_errors) suggest physical degradation. Aggressive scrubbing helps prevent uncorrectable errors
Elevated read/write error rates (IR:AF_read_errors, AF_write_errors) indicate strained NAND operations. Reduced throughput may prolong usability
Lack of historical trends requires frequent monitoring for accelerating degradation signals

atomic_claims.claim: Wear leveling count at 95% (endurance threshold)
60 media errors detected
80 read errors recorded
40 write errors observed
10,000 power-on hours operational history
Concurrent high read/write error rates
No error correction capability remaining (implied by static error counts)
